title: Classic brownies
subtitle: Fudgy, dense chocolate brownies with a shiny crinkled top.
excerpt: Dark chocolate and butter melted together, whisked with eggs and sugar until thick and shiny, then folded with flour and baked just short of set. The shiny top, the dense fudgy centre, the crackly edge. Makes sixteen.
sourceNotes: Chocolate brownies. The brownie originated in the United States in the late 19th century; the first published recipes appear in American cookbooks from around 1905. The fudgy, high-chocolate version became the dominant style in the 20th century. The shiny crinkled top crust is created by dissolving the sugar in the hot chocolate mixture, a technique now documented in modern pastry science.

content.text: Brownies are underbaked by design. Take them from the oven when the centre still has a wobble and the edges are just set; a skewer comes out with moist crumbs, not clean. They continue to set as they cool. A fully baked brownie is a dry brownie.

Heat the oven to 180°C. Grease and line a 20 cm square tin. Melt {{dark-chocolate}} of dark chocolate and {{unsalted-butter}} of butter together in a bowl over simmering water, or in a microwave in short bursts. Stir until smooth. Whisk in {{caster-sugar}} of sugar until dissolved. Whisk in {{eggs}} eggs one at a time, then add {{vanilla-extract}} of vanilla. Fold in {{plain-flour}} of flour, {{cocoa-powder}} of cocoa powder and {{sea-salt-fine}} of salt until just combined. Pour into the tin and bake for 22 to 25 minutes until the edges are set and the centre still wobbles when the tin is shaken.
Leave in the tin for at least 30 minutes before cutting. The brownie continues setting as it cools. Cut with a sharp knife, wiping the blade between cuts for clean edges.

items.fix: Bake to the wobble test, not until a skewer comes out clean. For fudgy brownies, under-baking is correct.
Whisk the eggs into the still-warm (not hot) chocolate mixture. The dissolved sugar and beaten eggs create the crust.
items.cause: Eggs not beaten in well, or chocolate mixture not warm when eggs added.

items.fix: Store in an airtight tin. A well-fudgy brownie keeps for 4 to 5 days without drying.
items.symptom: Dry and crumbly after a day

content.text: The brownie is one of the great American contributions to baking: simple and satisfying. The fudgy versus cakey debate is long-settled in home kitchens: fudgy wins. These brownies use enough chocolate to be properly intense rather than sweet, and enough butter to stay moist. They are better on day two, when the texture has fully settled.
